sourceRows.Title: Exceptional quantum geometry and particle physics
sourceRows.Summary: Argues that the Euclidean Albert algebra is relevant for particle internal space, with triality associated to three Standard Model generations and Jordan-module connections.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Internal-space and connection lead only; no observed W/Z/H mass rows, VEV, pole extraction, or GeV normalization.

sourceRows.Title: Deducing the symmetry of the standard model from the automorphism and structure groups of the exceptional Jordan algebra
sourceRows.Summary: Uses automorphism/structure groups of J3^8 and Borel-de Siebenthal subgroup theory to derive Standard Model symmetry structure.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Symmetry derivation lead; it does not provide a GU-local electroweak mass matrix or target-independent W/Z/H masses.

sourceRows.Title: Exceptional quantum geometry and particle physics II
sourceRows.Summary: Develops J3^8/J2^8 internal-space descriptions for three generations and one generation without introducing new fundamental fermions or electroweak-symmetry trouble.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Generation/electroweak representation lead; no physical W/Z source rows, scalar potential, or mass-scale lineage.

sourceRows.Title: The standard model, the Pati-Salam model, and Jordan geometry
sourceRows.Summary: Presents a Jordan-algebra framework that most nearly describes the Standard Model but actually yields a viable B-L/scalar extension and a Pati-Salam extension.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Geometric model-building lead; extra scalar/B-L structure is external and no observed W/Z/H mass law is supplied.

sourceRows.Title: Exceptional quantum algebra for the standard model of particle physics
sourceRows.Summary: Uses the exceptional Euclidean Jordan algebra, F4 subgroups, S(U(3)xU(2)), primitive idempotents, and triality to describe Standard Model internal structure and Higgs Yukawa coupling.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Higgs/Yukawa structural lead; does not derive Higgs self-coupling, W/Z mass matrix, observed projection, or GeV scale.

sourceRows.Title: The Standard Model, The Exceptional Jordan Algebra, and Triality
sourceRows.Summary: Relates the complexified exceptional Jordan algebra to the Standard Model, left-right symmetry, Spin(10), octonionic projective geometry, and three-generation triality.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Representation and generation lead; no target-independent boson-mass source lineage.

sourceRows.Title: Dixon-Rosenfeld Lines and the Standard Model
sourceRows.Summary: Uses Freudenthal-Tits construction and Jordan-algebra tensor products to uplift Standard Model interactions over Dixon-Rosenfeld projective-line analogues.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Magic-square/Jordan uplift lead; the paper says further representation work remains and Higgs interactions are future work.

sourceRows.Title: Fermion mass ratios from the exceptional Jordan algebra
sourceRows.Summary: Current 2026-revised exceptional-Jordan mass-ratio program using Peirce slots, triality, Sym^3 flavor structure, a cubic determinant, and an E6-invariant Yukawa.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Current mass-ratio lead is fermion-scoped; it does not provide W/Z absolute rows or a target-independent observed Higgs mass.

decision: Do not promote W/Z or Higgs physical masses from exceptional-Jordan, Albert-algebra, triality, or Freudenthal-Tits magic-square routes in this repository. The sources provide serious geometric and algebraic Standard Model structure leads, including internal-space models, electroweak subgroup embeddings, Higgs/Yukawa or scalar-extension clues, three-generation triality, and current fermion mass-ratio work. They do not provide a GU-local Jordan algebra map from Shiab/observer geometry, a target-independent electroweak mass matrix, separate W/Z source rows, observed photon/W/Z/H projection, Higgs scalar-source/self-coupling lineage, VEV or mass-scale source, RG/threshold and pole extraction, or GeV normalization.
nextRequiredArtifact: A GU-local derivation mapping Shiab/observer-sector fields into an exceptional-Jordan, Albert, or magic-square internal geometry without importing an external representation choice.
A target-independent Peirce/triality/branching theorem that produces observed electroweak SU(2) x U(1), photon/W/Z projection, and separate W/Z source rows.
A Higgs scalar-source operator plus self-coupling or excitation law from the same Jordan geometry, not only a Higgs/Yukawa representation or external B-L scalar extension.
A shared VEV or mass-scale source, low-energy RG/threshold transport, physical-pole extractor, and GeV normalization before the route can promote W/Z/H masses.
